issues.display_title: Issue #4083: Implement Clarify Free AI Tokens Conversion in AI Blur Modal
issues.main_description_html:
Description

A UX issue was reported where users (specifically new users) can successfully apply AI Blur even when their account appears to have 0 standard tokens. The system grants free AI tokens, but that balance is hidden, and the Order Summary modal silently folds it into the deduction — showing a confusing "Total Cost: 0 Tokens" with no indication of which tokens (free vs. paid) were consumed.

This task makes the Free AI Token balance visible and breaks the deduction down so the billing math is clear.

📐 Based on approved design: docs/brains/2026-06-17-ai-blur-free-token-balance-design.md (Status: Approved).
⚠️ Note: The design sets the conversion as 1 paid Token = 10 AI Tokens (the original ticket said "20"). The design value is authoritative.

Affected File

  • src/pages/Tour/Detail/Blur/Components/AIBlurModal.jsx

The API already returns v1CreditData.free_trial (the free AI token balance), but it is currently folded into effectiveCreditAvailable without being surfaced to the user.

Design Overview

New computed values (added after savedForLater, ~line 91)

  • freeCreditsAvailable — free AI tokens from v1CreditData.free_trial (only for isAllowedUser; else 0).
  • freeCreditsApplied — min(freeCreditsAvailable, blurringCost).
  • paidCreditsApplied — paid subscription credits consumed after free credits run out.

No changes to existing values (blurringCost, effectiveCreditAvailable, totalCost, tokenRequired, savedForLater).

Order Summary UI changes

  1. New "Free AI Tokens" balance row (only when isAllowedUser), inserted before the Blurring Cost row:
    • Chip is green with a gift icon (fa-gift) when freeCreditsAvailable > 0.
    • Chip is muted grey with a ban icon (fa-ban) when freeCreditsAvailable === 0.
    • ℹ️ tooltip text: "Free AI Tokens are given at no cost and are used first before your paid Tokens. 1 paid Token = 10 AI Tokens."
  2. Replace the "Available AI Tokens Used" row with conditional indented sub-rows for allowed users:
    • "Free Tokens Applied" (green, fa-gift) when freeCreditsApplied > 0.
    • "Paid Credits Applied" (amber, fa-coins) when paidCreditsApplied > 0.
    • Non-allowed users keep the original single row unchanged.

Behavior by scenario

Scenario Free AI Tokens chip Sub-rows shown
1 — All free (free covers full cost) 🟢 green, balance > 0 "Free Tokens Applied" only
2 — Mixed (free exhausted, subscription fills rest) 🟢 green, balance > 0 "Free Tokens Applied" → "Paid Credits Applied"
3 — No free (0 free tokens) ⬜ muted grey, balance = 0 Neither sub-row shown

i18n

Add four keys under t_panorama_tools.t_ai_blur.t_modal to all 18 locale files (ba, cs, da, de, en, es, fr, hi, it, jp, nl, pl, pt, ro, ru, sk, sv, zh); en.json is authoritative, others receive the English string until translated:

  • t_free_tokens_available → "Free AI Tokens"
  • t_free_tokens_available_tooltip → "Free AI Tokens are given at no cost and are used first before your paid Tokens. 1 paid Token = 10 AI Tokens."
  • t_free_tokens_applied → "Free Tokens Applied"
  • t_paid_credits_applied → "Paid Credits Applied"

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When isAllowedUser is true, a "Free AI Tokens" row appears above "Blurring Cost" in the order summary.
  2. When freeCreditsAvailable > 0, the chip is green with a gift icon (fa-gift).
  3. When freeCreditsAvailable === 0, the chip is muted grey with a ban icon (fa-ban).
  4. The ℹ️ tooltip reads: "Free AI Tokens are given at no cost and are used first before your paid Tokens. 1 paid Token = 10 AI Tokens."
  5. When free credits cover the full cost: only the "Free Tokens Applied" sub-row appears (green, indented).
  6. When cost exceeds the free balance and subscription credits fill the rest: both "Free Tokens Applied" (green) and "Paid Credits Applied" (amber) sub-rows appear.
  7. When freeCreditsAvailable === 0: neither sub-row appears.
  8. Non-allowed users see the original "Available AI Tokens Used" row unchanged.
  9. All 18 locale files have the four new i18n keys added.
  10. Dark mode is supported for all new elements (green/amber tonal variants).

Out of Scope

  • Changing how tokens are consumed or the order of credit deduction.
  • Showing the free token balance anywhere outside this modal.
  • Translating the four new keys into non-English locales.
  • Changes to isNoToken / isHasToken / isUnsubscribe state logic.

Reference: docs/brains/2026-06-17-ai-blur-free-token-balance-design.md (approved design) and docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md §5.2 (Token micropayments). Related to blur work #4072–#4075.

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.18-32-34.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4085: Integrate API Get Panorama Manual Blur
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Integrate the Get Panorama Manual Blur API into the dashboard's Anonymize 360 Images / Manual Blur flow.

This covers wiring the frontend to the GET endpoint that retrieves existing manual blur data for a panorama (e.g. from the 8_blooring table), so previously saved manual blur regions can be loaded back and displayed/edited in the UI.

Scope

  • Integrate the Get Panorama Manual Blur API endpoint to fetch existing manual blur data for a given panorama.
  • Load and render the retrieved blur regions in the Manual Blur / 360 Image Connector UI.
  • Handle API responses/states correctly (loading, empty/no-blur, success, error).
  • Reflect blur status accurately (e.g. the blue indicator on thumbnails that have manual blur data).

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Existing manual blur data for a panorama is successfully fetched via the API.
  2. Retrieved blur regions are correctly displayed in the UI (and available for editing where applicable).

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Tour editor: face/plate blurring). Companion to #4075 (Integrate API Manual Blur); related to #4073, #4074.

Demo from Developer

Screen.Recording.2026-06-18.160613.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4081: Handle Publish Button Stuck on Infinite "Validating..." State
issues.main_description_html:
Description

A bug has been reported on the Publish to Street View flow: after clicking Publish, the button enters a "Validating..." state and spins indefinitely — the validation never resolves to Success or Failure, blocking the user from publishing.

The reporter notes the token/scopes appear OK (valid streetviewpublish, userinfo.email, userinfo.profile, openid scopes), so this is a state-resolution / timeout-handling issue rather than an auth problem — the frontend fails to settle the validation state. Affected users are forced to manually push the tour, which is a problem when clients need it published urgently.

Reference Data

  • Moderation ID: 3559433
  • User ID: 3514
  • Symptom: "Validating..." button never resolves (infinite spinner).

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The validation process must reach a final resolution (Success or Failure) and must not remain stuck spinning indefinitely.
  2. If the validation fails or times out, the UI must display a clear error message, and the button must return to its active state so the user can retry.
  3. The tour can successfully proceed to the publish phase if the validation passes on the backend.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Publishing: Google Business / Street View Blue Line) and §10 ("Stuck publishing" failure mode).

Demo by Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #4084: Fix Issue Sub Location Not Working Correctly
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Bug Report: Sub Locations not working correctly (reported by a photographer, account user_id: 253).

The Sub Location management on the Photographer Location / Set Location page behaves inconsistently — duplicate entries appear, a false "maximum" error is triggered, and saved sub-locations don't match what the user entered after reload.

Initial Situation

User status is "pending". They want to set up the following locations:

  • Headquarters: Basel, Switzerland
  • Branch offices: Lucerne, Zurich, Bern

Steps / Observed Behavior (the bug)

  1. Lucerne appeared twice in the sub-location list. The user deleted the duplicate entry.
  2. Clicking "Next" triggered the error message: "Maximum 4 sublocations allowed per photographer" — even though only 3 branch offices were intended.
  3. After reloading the page, a new inconsistency appeared: Bern is now listed twice, and Lucerne is missing from the map.

The behaviour is inconsistent and cannot be reproduced in a controlled manner.

Expected Result

  • Headquarters: Basel, Switzerland
  • Branch offices: Lucerne, Zurich, Bern

(i.e. exactly the sub-locations the user entered — no duplicates, no missing entries, no false "maximum" error.)

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Sub-locations save exactly as entered — no duplicate entries are created or persisted.
  2. The "Maximum 4 sublocations allowed per photographer" validation only triggers when more than 4 sub-locations actually exist (deleted duplicates are not counted).
  3. After saving and reloading, the sub-location list and the map markers accurately reflect the user's intended entries (Basel HQ + Lucerne, Zurich, Bern) with no missing or duplicated pins.
  4. The behavior is consistent and reproducible (no state desync between the list, the saved data, and the map).

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §4 (Photographer profile & directory) and §12 (Maps). Affected user user_id: 253.

Demo by Developer

Desain.tanpa.judul.1.mp4

issues.display_title: Issue #4082: Add Status Toggle "Show Photographers Approved" to Featured Photographers Table
issues.main_description_html:
Description

To optimize the workflow for the Support team, we need to change the default view of the Featured Photographers page. Currently, the table loads all requests, causing it to be cluttered with already approved photographers.

New Logic

  • When a Support user enters the page, the table should only display "Pending" and "Denied" requests by default.
  • To view the approved ones, add a toggle switch above the table labeled "Show photographer approved".
  • The default state of this toggle is false.

Sub-tasks

  • Default State Logic: Update the initial data fetch/render logic on page load so that it filters out "Approved" requests. The table should only show items with a "Pending" or "Denied" status.
  • Add Toggle UI: Implement a toggle switch above the table with the label "Show photographer approved".
  • Toggle Action: Set the default value of the toggle to false. When the user toggles it to true, update the table state to include "Approved" photographers in the list.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. On page load, the Featured Photographers table shows only Pending and Denied requests (Approved are hidden by default).
  2. A "Show photographer approved" toggle is visible above the table and defaults to off (false).
  3. Toggling it on (true) includes Approved photographers in the table; toggling off hides them again — without a full page reload.

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §3 Persona D (Support/Admin) and §4 (Support / admin tooling: featured photographers).

Demo by Developer

2026-06-18.18-24-57.mp4
